input.content:
name: PR Label Assigner

# The workflow has two responsibilities:
#   1. Run the Pester suite for the script (unit + integration tests).
#   2. Drive the script end-to-end against fixture data and emit the
#      computed labels in a parseable form so the act-based test harness
#      can assert exact expected values.
#
# The fixture path is parameterised via the FIXTURE_DIR env var so the
# harness can rotate through several fixture sets without rewriting
# workflow files.

on:
  push:
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      fixture_dir:
        description: 'Fixture directory under fixtures/ (e.g. case-basic)'
        required: false
        default: 'case-basic'

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  FIXTURE_DIR: ${{ github.event.inputs.fixture_dir || 'case-basic' }}

jobs:
  test:
    name: Pester unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Check out repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Pester
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
          Import-Module Pester -MinimumVersion 5.0
          $config = New-PesterConfiguration
          $config.Run.Path = './tests'
          $config.Run.Throw = $true
          $config.Output.Verbosity = 'Detailed'
          Invoke-Pester -Configuration $config

  assign-labels:
    name: Assign labels from fixture
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Check out repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Resolve fixture
        id: fixture
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
          $dir = Join-Path 'fixtures' $env:FIXTURE_DIR
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $dir)) {
            throw "Fixture directory not found: $dir"
          }
          $files = Join-Path $dir 'changed-files.json'
          $rules = Join-Path $dir 'rules.json'
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $files)) {
            throw "Missing changed-files.json in $dir"
          }
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $rules)) {
            throw "Missing rules.json in $dir"
          }
          "files=$files" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append
          "rules=$rules" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append
          Write-Host "Using fixture: $env:FIXTURE_DIR"

      - name: Compute labels
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
          . ./src/Get-PRLabels.ps1
          $labels = Invoke-PRLabelAssignerFromJson `
            -ChangedFilesJsonPath '${{ steps.fixture.outputs.files }}' `
            -RulesJsonPath        '${{ steps.fixture.outputs.rules }}'

          # Emit two parseable forms so the harness can assert on either:
          #   1. One label per line, framed by sentinels.
          #   2. A single JSON line, also framed.
          Write-Host '---LABELS-BEGIN---'
          if ($null -ne $labels) {
            foreach ($l in $labels) { Write-Host $l }
          }
          Write-Host '---LABELS-END---'

          $json = if ($null -eq $labels) { '[]' } else { ($labels | ConvertTo-Json -Compress -AsArray) }
          Write-Host '---LABELS-JSON-BEGIN---'
          Write-Host $json
          Write-Host '---LABELS-JSON-END---'

# $env:FIXTURE_DIR directly, falling back to the workflow_dispatch input or
# 'case-basic' as a safe default.

jobs:
  test:
    name: Pester unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Check out repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Pester
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
          Import-Module Pester -MinimumVersion 5.0
          $config = New-PesterConfiguration
          $config.Run.Path = './tests'
          $config.Run.Throw = $true
          $config.Output.Verbosity = 'Detailed'
          Invoke-Pester -Configuration $config

  assign-labels:
    name: Assign labels from fixture
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Check out repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Resolve fixture
        id: fixture
        shell: pwsh
        env:
          INPUT_FIXTURE: ${{ github.event.inputs.fixture_dir }}
        run: |
          $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
          # Resolution order: act --env > workflow_dispatch input > default.
          $fixtureDir = $env:FIXTURE_DIR
          if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($fixtureDir)) {
            $fixtureDir = $env:INPUT_FIXTURE
          }
          if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($fixtureDir)) {
            $fixtureDir = 'case-basic'
          }
          $dir = Join-Path 'fixtures' $fixtureDir
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $dir)) {
            throw "Fixture directory not found: $dir"
          }
          $files = Join-Path $dir 'changed-files.json'
          $rules = Join-Path $dir 'rules.json'
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $files)) {
            throw "Missing changed-files.json in $dir"
          }
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $rules)) {
            throw "Missing rules.json in $dir"
          }
          "files=$files" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append
          "rules=$rules" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append
          Write-Host "Using fixture: $env:FIXTURE_DIR"

      - name: Compute labels
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
          . ./src/Get-PRLabels.ps1
          $labels = Invoke-PRLabelAssignerFromJson `
            -ChangedFilesJsonPath '${{ steps.fixture.outputs.files }}' `
            -RulesJsonPath        '${{ steps.fixture.outputs.rules }}'

          # Emit two parseable forms so the harness can assert on either:
          #   1. One label per line, framed by sentinels.
          #   2. A single JSON line, also framed.
          Write-Host '---LABELS-BEGIN---'
          if ($null -ne $labels) {
            foreach ($l in $labels) { Write-Host $l }
          }
          Write-Host '---LABELS-END---'

          $json = if ($null -eq $labels) { '[]' } else { ($labels | ConvertTo-Json -Compress -AsArray) }
          Write-Host '---LABELS-JSON-BEGIN---'
          Write-Host $json
          Write-Host '---LABELS-JSON-END---'
